The judge wishes him the best of luck, saying, "Go ahead, young man," as Kevin thanks him, and the audience erupts in more applause and cheers. The camera alternates between Kevin's expressive gestures, the judges' engaged reactions (including the third judge in a dark suit listening intently), and wide shots of the packed theater, maintaining a lively and energetic atmosphere. He addresses someone named "Heidi," asking her to "hold on your index finger" for him, stating it "is gonna help out a lot." As he instructs "Go like this," a distinct whooshing sound and a high-pitched, sustained electronic tone begin to build in the background. Kevin Li then dramatically declares "ET phone home," with the electronic tone intensifying. He continues, describing that he's "seeing a screen, not a small screen like a phone," but "larger," as the electronic sound persists. He then announces he's "smelling something buttery," prompting a judge, likely Judge 3, to exclaim "Whoa!" Kevin Li then directly asks the judges if they are "eating popcorn at the movie theater," leading to a collective "No!" from the wide-eyed, amused audience, accompanied by cheers, laughter, and applause. Judge 2 leans back, laughing heartily, while Kevin Li continues to engage the judges with expressive hand movements, leaning forward and pointing directly at Judge 2, who playfully points back. The tension builds, culminating in audience gasps and shouts of "Oh no!" and "No!", along with Kevin Li's own "Whoa!" and a woman's scream, as the electronic tone returns and crescendos. Kevin then stands at the judges' table, gesturing expressively while holding a small white object, which he reveals to be a folded piece of paper with 'CORE MEMORIES' written on it. He dramatically drapes his jacket over the easel, revealing the photos more clearly, as the background music shifts to a more emotional, reflective tone. Kevin speaks reflectively about memories, stating, "We tend to focus a lot on the future, but sometimes we forget about all the memories that made us who we are today." He then invites the judges to "rewind the hands of time," and as whimsical music plays, Judge 1 (Simon) exclaims "Whoa!" and Judge 2 delightedly recognizes, "Ah, it's us!" Kevin playfully adds, "Look how cute you all are! Even Simon!" prompting laughter from the audience and an amused chuckle from Simon. segments.integrated_caption: The video transitions from a dramatic performance to a lighthearted and whimsical act as Brynn Cummings, a young girl with long brown hair wearing a dark green dress with white heart patterns, confidently walks onto the stage holding a pink and white furry puppet with large blue eyes and a tuft of white hair. The stage is illuminated with vibrant blue and purple lights. The puppet, speaking in a high-pitched, slightly whiny voice, declares, "I don't wanna sing anymore," to which Brynn, speaking into a small microphone clipped to her dress, responds with exasperation, "What? This is what we've practiced for weeks." The puppet insists, "They wanna see something else, something new," prompting a chuckle from Judge 1 (the bald man). Brynn questions, "But we haven't practiced anything else. What's your plan?" The puppet excitedly exclaims, "I've got it! Mentalism!" Judge 1, surprised, echoes, "Mentalism?" and Brynn adds, "Wait, what?" as the audience claps and cheers. The puppet, dismissive, tells Brynn, "I don't mind, Reed. Quit playing around, let's just sing the song. Trust me!" It then announces, "Taking down to the judges." Brynn asks, "Is that allowed?" and Judges 3 (the man in the dark suit), 1, and 2 (the woman in the yellow top) all confirm with "Yeah," "Sure," and "Yeah" respectively, as Brynn walks toward the judges' table, holding the puppet. The audience erupts in cheers and applause, accompanied by light, magical background music. Judge 2, whose name is revealed to be Heidi, remarks, "She's so good," with Judge 1 agreeing, "He is good." Brynn expresses worry, "I have a bad feeling about this," but the puppet reassures her, "Oh, stop worrying." Brynn, frustrated, states, "Seriously, I don't know what I'm supposed to do." The puppet confidently declares, "It's okay, we'll use a deck of cards. Check your pocket," as the magical music continues. Brynn, confused, asks, "Wait, what?" before the puppet triumphantly announces, "Ta-da!" The audience reacts with delight. The puppet then addresses Judge 2, "Heidi, I'd like you to cut the deck one time," as Brynn stands beside the judges, the puppet perched on the table, and the audience applauds warmly, maintaining the cheerful, family-friendly atmosphere. Judge 2 reaches out to touch the puppet, and close-ups capture the puppet’s expressive face and Brynn’s animated expressions.

segments.integrated_caption: The video segment opens with a wide shot of the America's Got Talent stage, showcasing the judges' table with Simon Cowell, Sofia Vergara, and Heidi Klum seated, each with a Sonic-branded cup and a red buzzer. The audience is visible in the background, illuminated by blue and purple stage lighting. Mandy Muden, a woman with blonde hair wearing a leopard-print jacket over a black dress and a large white statement necklace, confidently walks onto the stage, carrying a beige handbag. She gestures expressively as she speaks, engaging with the audience and judges, humorously stating her age as "29," which elicits laughter from the audience. The camera cuts between close-ups of her animated performance and reactions from the judges and audience. Sofia Vergara, in a bright yellow top, asks Mandy, "what do you do, Mandy?" to which Mandy replies, "I do magic," prompting cheers. Sofia then inquires, "Why AGT? Now?" and Mandy, with a British accent, explains, "Well, being British, as Simon will back me up to say, I love America!" before comically adding, "Also I owe money and I had to get out the UK," drawing laughter from the judges and audience. Simon Cowell is seen smiling and nodding throughout. Sofia continues to playfully question Mandy, asking, "What are you gonna do with a million dollars if you win?" Mandy quips she'd "probably spend it on men," leading to Sofia's amused exclamation, "On men!" and further laughter. Sofia then encourages Mandy, saying, "Okay, we're ready for you. We want to see your magic," to which Mandy enthusiastically responds, "Okay, fantastic!" The audience cheers loudly as Mandy asks if they're "ready to get into a magic mood?" The stage backdrop features dynamic blue and gold geometric patterns, and the large screen behind the judges displays the 'America's Got Talent' logo with the names 'SIMON', 'SOFIA', 'HEIDI', and 'HOWIE' above their respective X buttons. Mandy concludes her introduction by observing the judges as "four strangers ready to judge me. It's like one of those parties where you throw the car keys into the fishbowl," generating more laughter. The segment culminates with Mandy walking toward the judges' table, where she is greeted warmly by Sofia Vergara, who stands and claps, while Simon Cowell and Howie Mandel (partially visible) also show approval. The atmosphere is lively and celebratory, with bright stage lights and a vibrant crowd response.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens on the brightly lit America's Got Talent stage, adorned with dynamic blue and gold geometric patterns and the show's logo on a large screen. Mandy Muden, a woman with blonde hair in a leopard-print jacket over a black dress and a large white statement necklace, begins her performance, holding a white sketchpad and black marker. She addresses the judges and audience, instructing them to 'read your mind, here we go,' before asking, 'Page 70. Does your word begin with an F?' Judge 2 (Sofia, in a bright orange top) laughs, and Mandy confirms, 'Yes!' She then asks if they are 'familiar with this word,' to which Sofia replies, 'Yes, I'm familiar with the word.' Mandy continues, stating, 'Now spirits are telling me that this word ends in I-N-G,' as she writes 'FING' on the pad and holds it up. The judges, including Judge 1 (bald man in black-rimmed glasses) smiling and Judge 3 (Howie, in a dark suit) laughing, react with increasing amusement. Mandy then asks, 'Have you done this word today?' Howie enthusiastically shouts, 'Yes!' Mandy exclaims, 'That's my girl! Fantastic!' as the audience, illuminated by blue stage lighting, claps and cheers. Mandy then reveals the full word, asking, 'Is your word by any chance, is it, um, fantasizing?' Sofia excitedly confirms, 'Yes, it is!' prompting a burst of cheers and applause from the engaged audience. Mandy then directs Sofia to 'go to 70' and 'look at the last word on page 70,' but Sofia humorously responds, 'I don't have my glasses.' Mandy then turns to Howie, asking, 'Howie, you've got a dictionary there?' to which he replies, 'Yes.' The lively and humorous atmosphere continues, with the judges seated at their table with Sonic cups, and the audience reacting enthusiastically to Mandy's engaging performance.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with Mandy Muden, a blonde woman in a leopard-print jacket, seated at the Judges' table, holding a large white sketchpad. With a clear, engaging female voice, she instructs Judge 1 (a bald man with a gray beard) to navigate to "page 330" and "seven word entries" in a dictionary. Judge 1, in a distinct male voice, confirms "Okay" and counts aloud before revealing the word "Looney." Mandy humorously exclaims "No!" as she writes 'FING' on her sketchpad, then corrects him with "It's Looney!", prompting building cheers and applause from the audience and swelling music. She then engages the other judges: asking Judge 3 (a man in a dark suit), identified as Simon, for his word ("Eat"), then Howie Mandel (also in a dark suit) for his letter ("It's an E"), and Judge 2 (a woman in a yellow top), identified as Heidi, who holds up a card with 'A' and states "I have an A." Mandy then asks Sofia (whose name is displayed on the stage screen) for her letter, to which she responds "A T." Mandy exclaims "Unbelievable!" as the audience erupts in enthusiastic applause and cheers, accompanied by triumphant music. Mandy stands, holding the sketchpad, and walks to the judges' table, handing the blue-covered 'Collins Pocket English Dictionary' to Judge 1. The judges, including Howie Mandel who exclaims "Wow!" and later remarks, "Mandy, that was quite a surprise!", are visibly entertained, laughing and gesturing. Heidi also adds, "You're so funny and I didn't..." The brightly lit 'America's Got Talent' stage, with its blue and gold geometric patterns and prominent logo, provides a lively backdrop, with Sonic cups remaining on the table throughout the humorous and engaging performance.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with Mandy Muden, a blonde woman in a leopard-print jacket over a black dress and a large white statement necklace, confidently performing on the brightly lit America's Got Talent stage, which features dynamic blue and gold geometric patterns and a large screen displaying the show's logo and judges' names. Mandy, speaking with an excited tone, exclaims, "It's great to have so much fun!" and addresses Judge 1, "Howie!" Judge 1, a bald man in a black denim jacket, identified as Howie, reacts with laughter and amusement, praising Mandy with a calm, appreciative voice: "You're a very good magician. You're funny, I love you. I'm a huge fan of yours. You are the epitome of somebody that deserves to have their own show. I would go see just you in Las Vegas." The audience, visibly entertained, claps enthusiastically as Mandy, overwhelmed, responds, "Yes! Oh wow, thank you so much! Thank you!" She then calls out, "Heidi!" to Judge 2, a woman in a bright orange top. Heidi, with a warm and playful voice, calls Mandy "adorable" and asks if she's "gonna keep this?" to which Mandy playfully replies, "Yes, of course!" Mandy then addresses Judge 3, "Simon!" Simon, a man in a dark long-sleeve shirt, smiles broadly and, with a distinct, slightly critical but ultimately appreciative voice, comments, "We saw everything, didn't we? I mean, you were mind-reading, you were funny, you're very naughty. I really like you." Mandy gratefully replies, "Oh, thank you!" before Simon adds, "I do." As the camera alternates between close-ups of Mandy's expressive face and wide shots of the stage, Mandy announces, "Now we're gonna vote!" She prompts Heidi, who enthusiastically declares, "It's a yes from me for Mandy!" Mandy thanks her, then turns to Simon, who confirms, "It's a yes." Finally, Mandy calls on Howie, who loudly exclaims, "Three yeses!" Amidst roaring audience applause and celebratory music, Mandy joyfully shouts, "Four yeses! Oh, thank you so much! That's amazing!" The segment culminates with the entire theater, including the judges, standing and applauding enthusiastically under vibrant purple and blue stage lighting, celebrating Mandy's successful act. Each judge has a branded Sonic cup with a red straw at their illuminated table.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with Mandy Muden, a woman with blonde hair wearing a leopard-print jacket over a black dress and a large white statement necklace, energetically walking across the America's Got Talent stage, waving to the audience amidst upbeat, celebratory music and applause. She approaches the Judges' table, saying "Thank you! Thank you!" Mandy engages in a playful interaction with Judge 1, a bald man with a gray beard, who laughs heartily and comments, "She is really good." Judge 2, a woman in a bright orange top, exclaims, "I'm absolutely in shock, that was amazing. I couldn't believe what Howie said, it was like, he's such a hero of mine. I was like, oh my god." Judge 3, a man in a dark long-sleeve shirt, is also seated at the Judges' table. Judge 1 adds, "She is great." Mandy responds with "Thank you, thank you so much. Thank you!" as Judge 2 echoes "Thank you!" The scene cuts to a wide shot of the stage, showing the full audience and the illuminated 'X' symbols beneath the Judges' table, as the applause fades slightly. The focus then shifts to a young boy named Ryland, introduced with a yellow nameplate, standing center stage in a teal polo shirt and brown pants, holding a piece of paper. He appears nervous but composed as Judge 2 warmly greets him, "Hello, welcome, welcome, welcome." The camera alternates between close-ups of Ryland and reactions from the judges, who are smiling and clapping. The audience is visibly engaged, with many clapping and smiling. Judge 2 asks, "What is your name?" to which Ryland, in a young boy's voice, responds, "Uh, my name is Ryland." Judge 2 then inquires, "How old are you?" and Ryland states, "I'm 10 years old." Judge 2 continues, "And, uh, where are you from?" Ryland replies, "I'm from England," which elicits cheers and applause from the audience. The stage is lit with dynamic blue and gold geometric patterns, and the America's Got Talent logo is prominently displayed on the backdrop screen. The judges remain attentive, with Judge 2 raising her hand in excitement and Judge 3 smiling broadly. Judge 2 playfully acknowledges, "Uh, so Ryland. Okay. Oh yes, yes, yes, yes, yes, yes, yes, he's from England, we know." Ryland takes a deep breath as Judge 2 asks, "Um, what is your talent, young man?" Ryland confidently declares, "Uh, well, I'm a magician." Judges react with an audible "Ooh!" and "Wow!" to his revelation. Judge 2 then asks, "How long you've been doing that?" Ryland replies, "Since I was two, so eight years," prompting another "Wow!" from the judges. The segment ends with Ryland standing confidently, holding his paper, as the audience continues to applaud.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens on the brightly lit America's Got Talent stage, adorned with blue and gold geometric patterns, as a young boy named Ryland, in a teal polo and brown pants, stands center stage holding a paper. He speaks nervously but clearly, introducing his act by stating, "What you're about to see is amazing, but I can't do it on my own. I need help." He then reveals, "Luckily, I have lots of friends that love magic just like me." The camera pans to the three judges at their table, each with a Sonic cup. Judge 2, a woman in a yellow top, exclaims "Oh, hello there!" as the audience claps and cheers, and Judge 1, a bald man in a black denim jacket, adds a "Wow." Ryland greets his "friends," a group of five young men in black shirts with names like 'JAKE' and 'MARK', who stand behind him holding white numbered cards. Ryland explains his act, instructing the judges to "pick one of my friends, pick one of the numbers and I'll place it in this holder," adding that "the choices are all yours." segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with Judge 2, a woman in a bright yellow top, smiling intently at the Judges' table. Ryland's clear voice explains, "Each of you had a free choice of which drawing you wanted, and more importantly, where to put it." He then points to the colorful hand-drawn cards—a snake, a lime, a house, and mountains—as he articulates, "The snake looks like an S, the lime looks like a C, the house looks like an A, and the mountains look like an M. That spells the word SCAM." Judge 1, the bald man, confirms with "Right?", followed by Judge 2's "Right," and Judge 3's "Yeah." Ryland continues, announcing they'll create a "brand new magic word," which elicits audience laughter and applause. He then instructs, "Everyone shout SCAM as loud as they can on the count of three. One, two, three..." The audience, illuminated by blue and purple stage lighting, erupts with a loud, unified "SCAM!", followed by more laughter and applause. Ryland then builds suspense, asking, "But what if I told you that we knew what the magic word would be before you even picked it?" He instructs the judges, "I'd like to take the drawing out of the envelope and show it to everyone," as suspenseful, upbeat music begins to play. Judge 2 holds up a lime card and Judge 3 a house card, mirroring the sequence Ryland arranged. The audience gasps and cheers as the judges reveal their matching cards, confirming the coordinated act. Ryland triumphantly declares, "As you can see, they match," as the music swells and the audience bursts into thunderous cheering and applause, fully immersed in the lively, interactive performance on the dynamic blue and gold geometric stage, complete with Sonic cups and illuminated 'X' symbols at the Judges' table.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens on the brightly lit America's Got Talent stage to the sound of audience applause and upbeat music. A young boy, Ryland, in a teal polo shirt, stands center stage, confidently explaining, "There's only something, something on the front of their t-shirts. My friends also had something on the back. Look." He directs five young men in black shirts, who hold numbered white cards. As the audience "oohs" and murmurs, Ryland declares, "Word equals scam," prompting a collective "Wow!" and laughter from the audience. He then shifts to a conspiratorial whisper, saying, "Okay, so I'm at AGT. I'm gonna go in, do what I need to do, get out as fast as I can," before urging, "Okay, let's go." He continues in an excited whisper, "I can't believe I'm actually inside the AGT theater. Right, there's a desk. Be quick. Can't get caught." Judge 1, a bald man in a black denim jacket, is seen watching intently and is heard asking, "What is he doing?" Ryland, still whispering, expresses concern, "Oh god, Simon's gonna hate this," followed by urgent, excited whispers like "Oh my god, you've got to warn... warn..." The group then rearranges the cards into illustrated ones depicting a snake, lime, house, hamburger, robot, and bee. Ryland addresses the judges directly, "Now, you're probably wondering what I did at the desk, Simon. Well, hopefully you're not too mad," before instructing, "Watch this." Dramatic music swells with audience cheers as the judges react with surprise and laughter. Judge 1 exclaims, "Whoa, it's a scam everywhere," as Ryland rapidly points to the cards, shouting, "Look, scam, scam, scam, scam, scam, scam, scam!" The segment ends with Ryland saying, "Sorry, Simon," as the judges' X buttons illuminate, indicating a positive reception.

segments.integrated_caption: The America's Got Talent stage opens with a wide shot, brightly lit with dynamic blue and gold geometric patterns, showcasing the judges' table where Judge 3 (a man in a dark suit), Judge 2 (a woman in a yellow top), and Judge 1 (a bald man) are seated, each with a Sonic cup. Magic Mike Jacobson, a man in a mustard-yellow jacket, white t-shirt, black pants, and white sneakers, stands center stage with an earpiece microphone. He begins speaking in a clear male voice, discussing whether he can 'quit,' which elicits an audible 'Oh!' and laughter from the audience. Judge 2, a woman with long blonde hair, chimes in with 'No pressure,' to which Magic Mike responds, 'Yeah, no big deal. But actually a huge deal for me.' Judge 1, a bald man with a gray beard, then asks, 'Well, let's see what you do. Are you nervous?' Judge 2 adds, 'Exactly, let's see.' Magic Mike admits, 'Yeah, I'm a little nervous. Dramatic music swells as the audience claps and cheers enthusiastically. Magic Mike gestures expressively as he moves toward the judges' table, announcing he will share something 'that every magician has, but no magician will show you for some reason.' He reveals, 'It's this. It's an invisible deck of cards,' acknowledging, 'Uh, it's a bit hard to see in this light.' He continues, explaining his trick: 'So, what I'm going to do is I'm going to burn the invisible deck from 52 all the way down to one.' He hands a small red and blue object to Judge 1, who examines it closely. Magic Mike then directly addresses Judge 1, identified as Simon, stating, 'At the end, Simon, you're going to be a hero, as you always are in my heart. segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of Judge 2, identified as Sofia, a woman in a bright yellow top with long blonde hair and prominent jewelry, holding a lit matchstick near her face with a focused, curious expression. Magic Mike Jacobson, in a mustard-yellow jacket, stands center stage, gesturing expressively as he explains the trick in a calm, clear male voice, stating, "Let's just say there's two groups. Number cards and face cards. We're going to decide whether you want to start to burn all the numbers or all the faces." He hands a small red and blue object to Sofia, who examines it closely while still holding the lit match. Judge 1, later identified as Simon, a bald man, and Judge 3, a man in a dark suit, are seen at the Judges' table with Sonic cups, observing intently. She then decisively chooses, "All the faces." Magic Mike Jacobson confirms, "The faces are now in the fire. You can pass the box over to Sofia," and further prompts, "Sofia, take your match, okay?" He continues to engage the judges, explaining, "Left in the fire we've got Jacks, Queens, Kings. The only way to divide those up would be, yeah, men and women," and instructs them to "burn that match." A sound of a match striking is heard. He then asks, "Do you want to burn all the men, or do you want to burn all the women?" Judge 3, leaning forward with curiosity, enthusiastically exclaims, "Oh, the men!" in a slightly deeper male voice. Magic Mike Jacobson responds, "That was a very quick decision. Go ahead and you'll blow it out." Sofia blows out the matchstick flame, her expression shifting to amusement. Magic Mike Jacobson smiles broadly, raising his hands in triumph as the audience begins to clap and cheer loudly. He then instructs, "And you can pass that to Simon," before concluding, "You're going to save one of the remaining cards for burning. All we have left are the four Queens. segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a close-up of Judge 1, a bald man with a gray beard and black-rimmed glasses, adjusting a microphone on the Judges' table, which features a white surface with a grid-patterned mat, a pen, and a branded Sonic cup. He wears a black denim jacket and a ring on his left hand. The scene quickly shifts to Magic Mike Jacobson, a man with short dark hair and a beard, wearing a mustard-yellow jacket over a white t-shirt, standing center stage in front of the Judges' table. He holds a small deck of cards and begins a magic performance, engaging the judges with expressive hand gestures, his confident voice instructing, "Now, take the card, slide it right back in. Put that all back in the box. And then throw it right back up to me whenever you're ready. Perfect." The background is a vibrant America's Got Talent stage with blue and gold geometric patterns and a large screen displaying a cityscape. As he shuffles the deck, the audience erupts in loud cheers and applause, to which Mike playfully responds, "Just, just minor miracles, it's fine." He then reveals a card with a red diamond symbol, places it on the table, and addresses Judge 1, now identified as Howie, "Howie, you shuffle the deck. You picked a card, you all saw it. For the very first time, what's the card that you picked?" Judge 1, Howie, responds clearly, "Ten of Clubs." Mike continues to interact with the judges, handing a card to Judge 1, who examines it with interest, while Mike clarifies, "You and I have never met, correct?" Howie confirms, "Never." Mike builds suspense, "For the very first time when I open this up, you said the Ten of Clubs, correct?" Howie replies, "I did." Mike then reveals, "Because every single card in here is face up, except for one," as dramatic, suspenseful music begins to build. The audience, visible in the background under blue and purple stage lighting, watches intently. The judges, including Judge 2 (a woman with long blonde hair in a bright yellow top) and Judge 3 (a man with short dark hair and a beard in a dark suit), are seated at the illuminated table with Sonic cups and microphones, leaning forward in curiosity. Judge 2 exclaims, "Uh-oh," in anticipation. Magic Mike Jacobson performs the trick, showing the card to the judges and then placing it back in the deck, creating a sense of mystery and engagement, announcing, "One card with a signature on it. Howie, can you confirm, is that your signature?" Howie, in disbelief, states, "It is." Mike instructs, "Will you flip that over for me?" to which Howie responds with utter surprise, "Are you kidding me?" Mike asks, "Is that your card?" The atmosphere is lively, with the judges reacting with astonishment and the audience erupting in a massive roar of applause and cheers. The performance culminates in Magic Mike Jacobson smiling broadly, concluding his act with a flourish and saying, "Thank you very much," as the audience continues to cheer loudly.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a close-up of a hand holding a playing card, which is then flipped to reveal a signature, before transitioning to a wide shot of the America's Got Talent stage. The audience erupts in loud cheers and enthusiastic applause, with someone exclaiming "Crazy!" as Magic Mike Jacobson, a man with short dark hair and a beard in a mustard-yellow jacket, gratefully says "Thank you, thank you." Upbeat, celebratory music begins, accompanying the camera's pan across the excited audience under vibrant blue and purple stage lighting. The host, a bald man in a dark suit, jokes, "And he kept all his clothes on!" to audience laughter. Magic Mike claps his hands together, smiling broadly, as the music continues. The host then asks, "Heidi, what do you think?" Judge 2, Heidi, in a bright yellow top, enthusiastically describes, "I mean, the audience was so quiet. I haven't heard this audience this quiet all day. I mean, we were all like [makes a gasping sound] like hanging by every word that was coming out of your mouth, and it was incredible." Magic Mike responds, "Thank you very much. Thank you." Judge 3, a man in a dark suit, adds, "Really cool." Heidi continues, "I'm like mesmerized. It was like fantastic. And it was funny at the beginning also with the invisible part." The host chimes in with "Good job!" and Magic Mike thanks him. Judge 1, Simon, a bald man in a black denim jacket, then makes a prediction in his distinct, confident voice: "No more advertising jobs for you. Because this is what I think you were born to do," adding "Genuinely. Thank you." The audience cheers "Yes!" Magic Mike expresses his gratitude, "Thank you so much." Simon further emphasizes the authenticity, stating, "I gotta say, and you know, people at home are always skeptical because they think I I I swear to you, nobody here was in on this. This is my signature." The stage, illuminated with dynamic blue and gold geometric patterns, and the large screen displaying the 'America's Got Talent' logo with the judges' names, underscore the celebratory and excited mood as Magic Mike Jacobson’s performance concludes to a standing ovation.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with the audience, illuminated by blue stage lighting, clapping and cheering enthusiastically. Judge 1, a bald man in a black denim jacket, praises the performance, stating, "incredible. I loved it, you know. I think it was really good and I think you're really good," before declaring, "I'm gonna start the voting with a yes!" The audience erupts in cheers. Judge 2, a woman in a bright yellow top, adds, "I say yes for me too," and when prompted with "Sophia?", she confirms with a clear "Yes!" Magic Mike Jacobson, a man with short dark hair and a beard, wearing a mustard-yellow jacket, stands center stage with a small earpiece microphone, performing a card trick with expressive gestures. The Host, a man in a dark suit, then announces, "And Simon? Four yeses!", as Magic Mike beams and repeatedly thanks everyone, saying, "Thank you. Thank you guys so much. Thank you. I appreciate it. Thank you. Thank you very much." Upbeat music swells as Magic Mike walks off the America's Got Talent stage, where he is greeted by the Host, who exclaims, "Magic Mike! Oh my god!" and asks, "How are you feeling? Tell me." Magic Mike responds, "I feel amazing. I feel like I've had so much stress all day and now I feel like so pumped." The camera follows him backstage, where he interacts with crew members. Meanwhile, the judges at their table, each with a Sonic cup, examine Polaroid-style photos Magic Mike handed them: Judge 2 receives a photo of hands holding a small object, and Judge 3 receives a photo of a person in a dark setting. Judge 2 exclaims, "That is crazy," and later, "That's crazy, Tom." Judge 1 asks, "Is it your signature?" and Magic Mike confirms, "That's my signature, too. It is my signature." Judge 1 adds, "It is crazy." Magic Mike then states, "I know, I want to keep this." The video concludes with a dynamic graphic of the 'America's Got Talent' logo, featuring the NBC and Peacock branding, with text promoting streaming and subscription options, as the music fades.
